goal: constraint generated assembly is the app
visual render pac-man board walls and dots
milestone buffered turn momentum with joystick and W/A/S/D keyboard fallback
program: implements goal "PAC-MAN BUFFERED TURN KEYBOARD FALLBACK"
start pac-man moving in a legal board-derived direction
keep pac-man moving in the current direction while the next board cell is traversable
read joystick port 2 at $dc00 when available
read c64 keyboard fallback W/A/S/D into the same requested direction buffer
buffer the latest requested player direction
change direction only when the buffered requested direction is legal at a board-cell center
do not auto-select turns at hallway ends
stop at a cell center if current direction is blocked and no buffered legal turn is available
move pac-man sprite pixel by pixel between board cell centers
choose direction sprite from current movement direction
clear dots as pac-man reaches board cells

spriteProjection.sizeTuning: smaller 10-12 pixel centered sprite body within 24x21 hardware sprite cell
spriteProjection.mouthDirection: hardware sprite pointer selected from current buffered-turn movement direction
spriteProjection.mouthAnimation: open and closed sprite frames alternate at a visible crunch pace while moving
spriteProjection.verticalSpriteArt: north/south mouth uses same radial sprite geometry as east/west
spriteProjection.spriteFootprint: all directions use shared center, shared radius, and shared mouth-wedge rule
spriteProjection.spriteGeometry: radial Pac-Man generated from one circle-like pixel model
spriteProjection.spriteOriginTuning: sprite y origin lowered by one pixel and x origin shifted left by three pixels for hallway centering
spriteProjection.spriteCopyFix: copies all 512 bytes for eight sprite frames
spriteProjection.speedTuning: two raster frames per interpolated pixel

joystickControl.movementPolicy: Pac-Man starts moving immediately, continues in the current legal direction, and applies buffered joystick or keyboard requested turns when legal at cell centers
joystickControl.wallPolicy: blocked requested turns are ignored; if current momentum is blocked and no buffered legal turn exists, Pac-Man stops
joystickControl.legalitySource: legal move masks generated from board.txt traversable cells
joystickControl.keyboardFallback: W/A/S/D keys update the same requested direction buffer as joystick input

pacmanPathWalker.turnPolicy: buffered joystick or keyboard requested legal turn, otherwise continue current legal direction
pacmanPathWalker.deadEndPolicy: start with a legal first target; continue current direction; stop at a cell center when current direction is blocked and no buffered legal turn exists

assemblyEfficiency.dataSource: compact board_render_row tables generated from board.txt
assemblyEfficiency.colorPolicy: derive blue/yellow color from compact render character value
